Greed involves a desire to acquire more than one needs, often at the expense of others. Although the Jewish person is taking advantage of a free meal, assigning the label “greedy” based solely on this behavior would be a moral judgment that the scenario does not definitively support. Likewise, the Protestant person’s generosity does not imply greed. Because the scenario does not provide enough evidence to conclusively identify greed, the safest answer is that it is unknown.
